title: Gymnanthes albicans
intro: Gymnanthes albicans is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, commonly known as White Gymnanthes or White Soapbark. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in treating various health conditions, including skin infections and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Gymnanthes albicans include triterpenoids and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, this plant is used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, and are also used in some local traditional medicine practices.
taxonomy: Gymnanthes albicans is classified within the family Rutaceae and is a species of flowering plant. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and its relationship with other plants in the Rutaceae family. The Rutaceae family, commonly known as the citrus family, includes many economically important plants such as citrus fruits and their relatives. Gymnanthes albicans is further classified within the genus Gymnanthes, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Caribbean and Central America. The species is also known by the synonym Zanthoxylum albicans, which reflects its close relationship with the Zanthoxylum genus.
